form: Revised Oswestry Back Pain Disability Questionnaire

questions.text: Rate your current back pain intensity

choices.A: I can tolerate the pain I have without having to use pain medication
choices.B: The pain is bad but I manage without taking pain medication
choices.C: Pain medication gives complete relief from pain
choices.D: Pain medication gives moderate relief from pain
choices.E: Pain medication gives very little relief from pain
choices.F: Pain medication has no effect on the pain and I do not use it

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to take care of yourself (washing, dressing)?

choices.A: I do not have to change my way of washing or dressing in order to avoid pain
choices.B: I do not normally change my way of washing or dressing even though it causes some pain
choices.C: Washing and dressing increase the pain but I manage not to change my way of doing it
choices.D: Washing and dressing increases the pain and I find it necessary to change my way of doing it
choices.E: Because of the pain, I am unable to do some washing and dressing without help
choices.F: Because of the pain, I am unable to do any washing or dressing without help

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to lift heavy weights?

choices.A: I can lift heavy weights without extra pain
choices.B: I can lift heavy weights but it gives extra pain
choices.C: Pain prevents me from lifting heavy weights off the floor but I can manage if they are conveniently positioned, e.g. on a table
choices.D: Pain prevents me from lifting heavy weights but I can manage light to medium weights if they are conveniently positioned
choices.E: I can only lift very light weights
choices.F: I cannot lift or carry anything at all

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to walk?

choices.A: Pain does not prevent me from walking any distance
choices.B: Pain prevents me from walking more than 1 mile
choices.C: Pain prevents me from walking more than 1/4 mile
choices.D: Pain prevents me from walking more than 100 yards
choices.E: I can only walk using a stick or crutches
choices.F: I am in bed most of the time and have to crawl to the toilet

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to sit?

choices.A: I can sit in any chair as long as I like
choices.B: I can only sit in my favorite chair as long as I like
choices.C: Pain prevents me sitting more than 1 hour
choices.D: Pain prevents me from sitting more than 1/2 hour
choices.E: Pain prevents me from sitting more than 10 minutes
choices.F: Pain prevents me from sitting at all

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to stand?

choices.A: I can stand as long as I want without extra pain
choices.B: I can stand as long as I want but it gives me extra pain
choices.C: Pain prevents me from standing for more than 1 hour
choices.D: Pain prevents me from standing for more than 1/2 hour
choices.E: Pain prevents me from standing for more than 10 minutes
choices.F: Pain prevents me from standing at all

questions.text: How does back pain affect your sleep?

choices.A: Pain does not prevent me from sleeping well
choices.B: I can sleep well only by using tablets
choices.C: Even when I take tablets I have less than 6 hours sleep
choices.D: Even when I take tablets I have less than 4 hours sleep
choices.E: Even when I take tablets I have less than 2 hours sleep
choices.F: Pain prevents me from sleeping at all

questions.text: How does back pain affect your social life?

choices.A: My social life is normal and gives me no extra pain
choices.B: My social life is normal but increases the degree of pain
choices.C: Pain has no significant effect on my social life apart from limiting my more energetic interests, e.g. dancing, etc.
choices.D: Pain has restricted my social life and I do not go out as often
choices.E: Pain has restricted my social life to my home
choices.F: I have no social life because of pain

questions.text: How does back pain affect your ability to travel?

choices.A: I can travel anywhere without extra pain
choices.B: I can travel anywhere but it gives me extra pain
choices.C: Pain is bad but I manage journeys over 2 hours
choices.D: Pain restricts me to journeys of less than 1 hour
choices.E: Pain restricts me to short necessary journeys under 30 minutes
choices.F: Pain prevents me from traveling except to the doctor or hospital

choices.A: My pain is rapidly getting better
choices.B: My pain fluctuates, but overall is definitely getting better
choices.C: My pain seems to be getting better but improvement is slow
choices.D: My pain is neither getting better nor worse
